summary: Regression + new-feature testing for GRAV-SHIFT share-card and Capacitor wrapper. Backend 20/20 pass. Frontend: menu, play, HUD, pause/resume, mute, leaderboard fetch+render, gameover panel, name input, submit-blocked-when-zero, share/download buttons present with correct labels and disabled state at score=0, no console errors. capacitor.config.json + /app/frontend/android scaffolds + PLAY_STORE_GUIDE.md all present and well-formed. Could not automate non-zero-score path in Playwright (timing-sensitive arcade gameplay), so the ACTIVE download/share click was code-reviewed only.
action_items: (Optional) When E1 next iterates, consider exposing a test-only window hook (e.g. window.__GRAVSHIFT_TEST_SETSCORE) gated behind a query-param so headless Playwright can reliably trigger gameover with a non-zero score and exercise the actual download/share click. Disabled-state and labels are already verified.
critical_code_review_comments: shareCard.js correctly resolves Promise<Blob> from canvas.toBlob('image/png'); fallback chain in shareCardBlob is robust (Capacitor -> navigator.canShare files -> navigator.share text -> downloadBlob).
Game.jsx generateAndShare wraps buildShareCard + downloadBlob in try/catch and surfaces share-status; share-status auto-clears at 2400ms.
Submit-score block at score===0 enforced both in JSX disabled prop AND visually OK; same logic applied to share/download buttons.
After successful submit-score, code re-fires GET /api/scores/rank to refresh globalRank (Game.jsx lines 644-647) — verified in code; not automated due to score=0 in test.
capacitor.config.json: appId=com.emergent.gravshift, appName=GRAV-SHIFT, webDir=build all correct.
/app/frontend/android scaffolded properly: build.gradle, app/, settings.gradle, gradlew, capacitor.settings.gradle present.
PLAY_STORE_GUIDE.md is comprehensive, covers Capacitor sync, signed AAB build, Play Console publish, $0.99 paid setup, internal-testing flow.

MINOR: animation loop runs at 60fps via requestAnimationFrame even on menu/gameover (re-renders avoided since draw() is ref-only — fine, but could add early-return when phase==='leaderboard' to save a tiny bit of CPU).
MINOR: in shareCardBlob.js the Filesystem dynamic import is unused (only Share.share is invoked) — dead-code; safe to remove.

success_rate.frontend: Regression flows (menu, HUD, pause/resume, quit, leaderboard fetch, gameover panel, name input, submit disabled at 0, share/download buttons present + disabled at 0, no console errors): 100% pass. Active share/download click path: not automated (gameplay too hard to script reliably) — verified by code review only.

context_for_next_testing_agent: Headless Chromium cannot easily survive past the first pipe in this canvas arcade game (pipeSpacing=280, pipeSpeed=200, gap=200; bird at x=160). Naive Space-key flapping at 150-300ms intervals reliably produces score=0. To validate the active download/share click in future runs, either (a) E1 adds a test-only window helper to bump score before triggering gameover, or (b) the agent uses page.evaluate to call generateAndShare directly via a React ref hook. The buttons + disabled-state + share-status DOM are all confirmed wired correctly. Backend test file: /app/backend/tests/test_gravshift_api.py — keep all 20 tests passing.
rca of the issue: No bugs found. The only friction was Playwright's inability to script reliable scoring in a fast-paced canvas game; this is a test-tooling limitation, not an app bug.
